mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d returns (win rate < 40%) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during periods of stable inflation reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5d returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, breaking the regime condition.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy suppresses equity valuations and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal income accelerates indicates profit margin compression and reduced corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 5d returns (win rate < 45%) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-recession recovery phases indicates strong capacity utilization and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 20d returns in > 50% of post-recession periods, invalidating the regime-specific signal.
